title: CRISPR Editing of Immortalized Cells with RNPs using Lipofection
author: Synthego
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.uibeuan

steps.title: Seed cells and incubate in 37°C/5%...
steps.instruction: Seed cells and incubate in 37°C/5% CO 2 incubator overnight so that they are 30-70% confluent on the day of transfection (Day 2). Undetermined, 5% CO2 incubator

steps.title: Dilute sgRNA and Cas9 to 3...
steps.instruction: Dilute sgRNA and Cas9 to 3 μM stock concentrations (3 pmol/μl). If you followed the Synthego QuickStart Guide , you may have 30 μM stock concentrations (30 pmol/μl), so be sure to dilute further to 3 μM (3 pmol/μl).

steps.title: Prepare RNPs in microcentrifuge tube (Tube 1)
steps.instruction: Prepare RNPs in microcentrifuge tube (Tube 1). Use the quantities (per reaction) in the table below. * You may need to experimentally determine the optimum amounts of sgRNA and Cas9 nuclease. Synthego recommends a ratio of 1.3:1 sgRNA to Cas9 for RNP formation. Note: For knock-ins, see Synthego's Nucleofection or Electroporation protocol.

steps.title: Incubate RNPs for 5-10 minutes at room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate RNPs for 5-10 minutes at room temperature. 00:05:00, Incubation

steps.title: In a separate microcentrifuge tube (Tube...
steps.instruction: In a separate microcentrifuge tube (Tube 2), dilute Lipofectamine™ CRISPRMAX™ Reagent in Opti-MEM™ I Reduced Serum Medium. Use the quantities (per reaction) in the table below. a undetermined, Opti-MEM I Reduced Serum Medium undetermined, CRISPRMAX Reagent

steps.title: Incubate transfection solution for 5 minutes at room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate transfection solution for 5 minutes at room temperature. 00:05:00, Incubation

steps.title: Add the transfection solution (Tube 2)...
steps.instruction: Add the transfection solution (Tube 2) directly to RNPs (Tube 1), and mix well by pipetting up and down.

steps.title: Incubate for 5-10 minutes at room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate for 5-10 minutes at room temperature. Do not exceed 30 minutes. 00:05:00, Incubation Synthego highly recommends reverse transfection (RNPs are added to wells first and cells are added second), as this method has resulted in high editing efficiencies.

steps.title: For suspension cells, resuspend in growth medium and mix well
steps.instruction: For suspension cells, resuspend in growth medium and mix well. Skip steps 9 and 10 below and proceed to step 11. Wash cells with 1X PBS (enough to cover bottom of each well), then aspirate PBS.

steps.title: Add trypsin (enough to cover bottom...
steps.instruction: Add trypsin (enough to cover bottom of each well), incubate for 5 minutes in a humidified 37°C/5% CO 2 incubator. Resuspend cells in an equivalent volume of medium to stop the trypsin reaction. 00:05:00, Incubation

steps.title: Transfer 0
steps.instruction: Transfer 0.42 – 1.2 x 10 5 cells per reaction to a microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge cells at 200 x g for 5 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge cells at 200 x g for 5 minutes. 00:05:00, Centrifugation

steps.title: Resuspend cells in 500 μl of the growth medium
steps.instruction: Resuspend cells in 500 μl of the growth medium. undetermined, growth medium

steps.title: Add the RNP-transfection solution mixture to...
steps.instruction: Add the RNP-transfection solution mixture to each well of a 24-well tissue culture plate (see table below).Add cell suspension to each well, and mix by pipetting (see table below). a undetermined, RNP-Transfection Solution undetermined, Cell suspension in growth medium

steps.title: Incubate cells for 2-3 days in...
steps.instruction: Incubate cells for 2-3 days in a humidified 37°C/5% CO 2 incubator. Undetermined, Incubation 48:00:00, Incubation

steps.title: Conduct analyses to determine editing efficiency
steps.instruction: Conduct analyses to determine editing efficiency: PCR, Sanger sequencing, and ICE analysis . Next-Gen Sequencing, FACS, or functional tests may be conducted as alternatives. Option : If storing cells for future use is desired, split cells into two groups (one for analysis and one for cell culture). Representative Data
